summary: Kenneth Suna demonstrates his covered call strategy on a $50k+ portfolio, generating $600-$1,400 weekly (~$50-$70k annually) with minimal time investment. He outlines his Sunday night research phase (20-30 min) followed by Monday market-open execution (30 min), emphasizing the risk-reward tradeoff of the strategy.
takeaways: Research phase on Sunday night takes 20-30 minutes before market open
Monday morning execution at 9:30am market open takes approximately 30 minutes
Consistent average weekly income of $1,000 from covered calls
Total weekly time commitment under one hour for $50-$70k annualized income
Kenneth generates $600-$1,400 per week selling covered calls
Strategy requires re-evaluating positions based on news and upcoming earnings announcements

strategy_candidates.change: Apply Kenneth's Sunday night pre-market research phase (20-30 min) plus Monday execution (30 min) to Brian's Premium Desk workflow to systematize the $1,250/mo premium target
strategy_candidates.evidence: Kenneth demonstrates a repeatable 1-hour weekly process generating $1,000/week average; Brian's $1,250/mo ($288/week) target is conservative relative to Kenneth's $600-1,400/week production, suggesting process optimization could help reach or exceed goals

title: Are covered calls worth it? #shorts
